paragraphs.text: Bank of America Corporation, through its subsidiaries, provides banking and financial products and services for individual consumers, small and middle-market businesses, institutional investors, large corporations, and governments worldwide. Its Consumer Banking segment offers traditional and money market savings accounts, certificates of deposit and IRAs, noninterest-and interest-bearing checking accounts, and investment accounts and products; and credit and debit cards, residential mortgages, and home equity loans, as well as direct and indirect loans, such as automotive, recreational vehicle, and consumer personal loans. The company's Global Wealth & Investment Management segment offers investment management, brokerage, banking, and trust and retirement products and services; and wealth management solutions, as well as customized solutions, including specialty asset management services. Its Global Banking segment provides lending products and services, including commercial loans, leases, commitment facilities, trade finance, and commercial real estate and asset-based lending; treasury solutions, such as treasury management, foreign exchange, and short-term investing options and merchant services; working capital management solutions; and debt and equity underwriting and distribution, and merger-related and other advisory services. The company's Global Markets segment offers market-making, financing, securities clearing, settlement, and custody services, as well as risk management products using interest rate, equity, credit, currency and commodity derivatives, foreign exchange, fixed-income, and mortgage-related products. As of December 31, 2021, it served approximately 67 million consumer and small business clients with approximately 4,200 retail financial centers; approximately 16,000 ATMs; and digital banking platforms with approximately 41 million active users. The company was founded in 1784 and is based in Charlotte, North Carolina.

paragraphs.text: Bank of America continues to demonstrate investment banking competitiveness through major transaction participation. On October 21, 2025, the bank was named among the arrangers for a $12.25 billion debt facility supporting Blackstone and TPG's Hologic buyout, working alongside Citigroup and Barclays. This transaction underscores BAC's positioning in large-scale corporate finance deals during Q3 2025, a period when peer institutions including JPMorgan and Goldman Sachs reported record investment banking fees.

paragraphs.text: On the consumer engagement front, Bank of America announced its fourth annual More Rewards Day scheduled for November 6, 2025, offering consumer and business credit cardholders a full day of bonus rewards on purchases. The program has accumulated significant traction, with cardholders on pace to earn approximately $100 million in extra rewards since inception, having already earned $74 million during the first three annual events. This initiative supports the bank's retail banking franchise amid a backdrop where bank ETFs have declined over the past month on credit jitters.

paragraphs.text: Competitive dynamics in wealth management remain active, with UBS recruiting two Merrill Lynch teams managing a combined $1 billion in assets as of October 20, 2025. This represents a reversal of fortune for UBS following recent high-profile departures, though it highlights ongoing talent competition in the brokerage channel where Bank of America's Merrill Lynch platform operates. Meanwhile, broader credit market concerns have emerged, with analysts noting growing cracks in credit markets that could impact equity valuations going forward.

paragraphs.text: Bank of America Corporation operates as a diversified banking and financial services institution serving individual consumers, small and middle-market businesses, institutional investors, large corporations, and governments worldwide. The company generates revenue through four primary segments: Consumer Banking (offering deposit accounts, credit/debit cards, residential mortgages, and consumer loans), Global Wealth & Investment Management (providing investment management, brokerage, banking, and trust services), Global Banking (commercial lending, treasury solutions, and advisory services), and Global Markets (market-making, securities clearing, and risk management products). As of December 31, 2021, the bank served approximately 67 million consumer and small business clients through approximately 4,200 retail financial centers, 16,000 ATMs, and digital banking platforms with approximately 41 million active users. The company employs 213,000 full-time employees and is headquartered in Charlotte, North Carolina.

evidence_appendix.text: Bank of America Corporation (Ticker: BAC) | Exchange: NYSE | Sector: Financial Services | Industry: Banks - Diversified | Market Cap: N/A | CEO: Brian Thomas Moynihan | Full-Time Employees: 213000 | IPO Date: 1973-02-21 | Country: US | Website: https://www.bankofamerica.com

Bank of America Corporation, through its subsidiaries, provides banking and financial products and services for individual consumers, small and middle-market businesses, institutional investors, large corporations, and governments worldwide. Its Consumer Banking segment offers traditional and money market savings accounts, certificates of deposit and IRAs, noninterest-and interest-bearing checking accounts, and investment accounts and products; and credit and debit cards, residential mortgages, and home equity loans, as well as direct and indirect loans, such as automotive, recreational vehicle, and consumer personal loans. The company's Global Wealth & Investment Management segment offers investment management, brokerage, banking, and trust and retirement products and services; and wealth management solutions, as well as customized solutions, including specialty asset management services. Its Global Banking segment provides lending products and services, including commercial loans, leases, commitment facilities, trade finance, and commercial real estate and asset-based lending; treasury solutions, such as treasury management, foreign exchange, and short-term investing options and merchant services; working capital management solutions; and debt and equity underwriting and distribution, and merger-related and other advisory services. The company's Global Markets segment offers market-making, financing, securities clearing, settlement, and custody services, as well as risk management products using interest rate, equity, credit, currency and commodity derivatives, foreign exchange, fixed-income, and mortgage-related products. As of December 31, 2021, it served approximately 67 million consumer and small business clients with approximately 4,200 retail financial centers; approximately 16,000 ATMs; and digital banking platforms with approximately 41 million active users. The company was founded in 1784 and is based in Charlotte, North Carolina.
